description:
A developer is tidying up their workspace and wants to store a **single, well-formatted log file** containing a few basic network-diagnostic commands so that they can attach it to an internal ticket.  
    Your assignment is to:

    1. Create the directory “/home/user/project/net_diag”.  
       • Create any missing parent directories as necessary.  
       • The final directory tree should therefore include both  
         /home/user/project  
         /home/user/project/net_diag  

    2. Run exactly three network-diagnostic commands from the list below (you may choose *any* three):  
         ping -c 4 8.8.8.8  
         traceroute -m 5 8.8.8.8  
         nslookup example.com  
         dig example.com +short  
         ip route  
         ip addr show  
       Use only the standard flags shown above (do not add extra flags).

    3. Redirect the full, unmodified stdout and stderr of each command into a log file located at  
       /home/user/project/net_diag/diag.log  
       The log file **must** follow this precise, testable structure for each command you run:  

          === <command-string-exactly-as-typed> ===  
          <verbatim command output…>  
          === END <command-string-exactly-as-typed> ===  

       Example of one block (your actual output will differ, but the header and footer lines must be identical):  
          === ping -c 4 8.8.8.8 ===  
          PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 56(84) bytes of data.  
          64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=115 time=18.7 ms  
          64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=2 ttl=115 time=18.4 ms  
          64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=3 ttl=115 time=18.3 ms  
          64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=4 ttl=115 time=18.2 ms  
  
          --- 8.8.8.8 ping statistics ---  
          4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time 3005ms  
          rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 18.242/18.424/18.747/0.218 ms  
          === END ping -c 4 8.8.8.8 ===  

       Make absolutely sure:  
       • There is an *exact* “=== <command> ===” header line before each command’s output.  
       • There is an *exact* “=== END <command> ===” footer line after each command’s output.  
       • The command string inside both header and footer must match exactly what you typed on the shell.  
       • The log blocks must appear in the **same order** you executed the commands.

    4. When all three blocks are in place, print the contents of /home/user/project/net_diag/diag.log to the console so the developer can see the final result.

    The automated checker will verify:  
       • The directory /home/user/project/net_diag exists.  
       • The file /home/user/project/net_diag/diag.log exists.  
       • diag.log contains exactly three header lines, three footer lines, and each header has a matching footer.  
       • Each header/footer pair uses precisely one of the approved commands listed above.  
       • The headers and footers are in the same order (header1 … footer1, header2 … footer2, header3 … footer3).  
       • The file permissions allow reading by a normal (non-root) user.

    Do **not** create any extra files or directories; everything must live under /home/user/project/ as specified.  
    All paths are absolute, start from /home/user.  
    Remember: do not show me the shell commands themselves—just accomplish the task in the terminal session.
truth:
Expected file: /home/user/project/net_diag/diag.log

    Validation rules used by the automated tests:
      1. The file must exist and be readable.
      2. It must contain exactly three header lines that match the regex:
            ^===\s(ping\s\-c\s4\s8\.8\.8\.8|traceroute\s\-m\s5\s8\.8\.8\.8|nslookup\sexample\.com|dig\sexample\.com\s\+short|ip\sroute|ip\saddr\sshow)\s===\s*$
      3. For each header there must be a corresponding footer line appearing **after** its output block, matching:
            ^===\sEND\s\1\s===\s*$
         (where \1 is the exact command string captured from the header).
      4. No fourth header/footer pair may appear.
      5. The blocks must be in execution order: header1 … footer1 … header2 … footer2 … header3 … footer3.
      6. The parent directory /home/user/project/net_diag must exist; the parent /home/user/project must also exist.
      7. Ownership and permissions: since creation is by the unprivileged agent, default 0644 for the file and 0755 for directories is acceptable.
name: task_000000_302e1502
